items.title: NT Health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across the outback

children.content: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A doctor with his arms folded stands in front of a window

articles.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

image.altText: A young child gets there nose blown.

articles.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

gallery.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

image.altText: Albo has arms around an Indigenous woman wearing scarf, holding pink toy, a woman has head on another's shoulder. All serious.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies

children.content: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

image.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

items.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID

children.content: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.

image.altText: Crowd of people wearing face masks walking across a scramble crossing in Brisbane CBD.

items.title: NT Police officer acquitted of coercing ex-partner over T-shirt assault

children.content: A Northern Territory Police officer has been cleared of coercing his former partner into withdrawing an assault charge after facing trial on one count of attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A group of people in suits walking out of the NT Supreme Court.

items.title: How a 'partial obscuration' phenomenon is impacting Darwin's sunsets

children.content: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

gallery.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

thumbnail.altText: A pink and orange sunset, viewed from a balcony, is intersected by a beam of blue sky.
Large clouds obscure the setting sun, casting shadows of blue sky over the rays of sunlight.
A photo of sunset reflected in the water of a rock pool, it has beams of blue sky and pink light.

items.title: Man charged with Arnhem Land murder bailed to live with family 500km away

children.content: A 20-year-old man charged with the murder of another man during a brawl in Arnhem Land last month has been bailed to live with family far from the location of the death, in the Tiwi Islands.

image.altText: An aerial view of Milingimbi community in north-east Arnhem Land.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

image.altText: Aboriginal woman, brown/gray hair tied back, blue pattern deress, serious expression, sitting, blurred greenery behind her

items.title: 'Survival mode all the time': Australian parents break taboos on raising kids

children.content: Almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national survey of parents as the birth rate hits a record low

children.content: David Brat, who rose to political prominence as part of the conservative Tea Party movement, takes questions from senators at a confirmation hearing.

image.altText: David Brat sits at a desk and speaks.

children.content: The move is the latest escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government.

items.title: Aged care refunds for overcharging a 'burden' and 'total joke,' advocates say

children.content: Some advocates say the government's decision to delay price caps for at-home aged care services is a "burden".

image.altText: A man wearing a collared shirt and cardigan sits in a house. He looks into camera with a serious expression.

items.title: They might look the same, but these penguins are very different

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

gallery.altText: Three small penguins with bright red-orange bills and conspicuous white eye patches.
A woman in a purple beanie standing in front of a colony of king penguins.
A penguin colony in an Antarctic landscape.

A penguin colony in an Antarctic landscape.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: Can The Mandalorian and Grogu save Star Wars?

children.content: Grogu (aka Baby Yoda) made The Mandalorian a hit on streaming, now Disney is looking to parlay that goodwill into ticket sales to the latest Star Wars cinematic release.

image.altText: Four small creatures ride in a cart in the desert

items.title: How to improve your home without stepping inside

children.content: Adding a new kitchen or bathroom to your home can be expensive, but budget constraints don't mean you have to take home improvement off the table altogether.

items.title: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image has been revealed. Here's why

children.content: The youngest of the Snowtown killers was the subject of a long-running suppression order that kept his face — or any description of his appearance — hidden for 25 years. Here's why that's been lifted.

items.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'

children.content: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

items.title: Recycling contractor accused of having employees sleep on floor sues for $5m

children.content: Allegations that a depot operator in Queensland's container exchange recycling program conducted itself in a manner that could be likened to modern slavery have been detailed during a court hearing in a multi-million dollar federal court dispute.

image.altText: A birds eye view of green sheds.

items.title: Pendlebury's 433rd game simply his latest of many AFL records

items.title: A year on, survivors still counting cost of '1-in-500-year' disaster

children.content: It was six months before Ed Trotter could return home after record floods on the NSW Mis North Coast. A year on from the disaster, others are still without working kitchens and bathrooms.

items.title: Victorian town braces for impact of impending Bega Cheese factory closure

children.content: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

items.title: Trawlers may sit idle but residents are holding onto Karumba's future

children.content: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".
